markdown.markdown:
Flow Matching models the unresolved residual after the CNO mean:

$$
\mathbf{x}_1 = \mathbf{x}_{HR} - \boldsymbol{\mu}
$$

The model starts from noise:

$$
\mathbf{x}_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(0, I)
$$

and learns a velocity field that moves from noise to the residual:

$$
\mathbf{x}_t = (1-t)\mathbf{x}_0 + t\mathbf{x}_1
$$

For the linear path used in the v4 family, the target velocity is:

$$
\mathbf{v}^{*} = \mathbf{x}_1 - \mathbf{x}_0
$$

The training objective is:

$$
\mathcal{L}_{FM} =
\mathbb{E}_{t,\mathbf{x}_0,\mathbf{x}_1}
\left[
\left\| v_{\phi}(\mathbf{x}_t, t, \boldsymbol{\mu}, \mathbf{x}_{LR})
- (\mathbf{x}_1 - \mathbf{x}_0) \right\|_2^2
\right]
$$

## Inference

At inference time the model samples a new residual by solving an ODE:

$$
\frac{d\mathbf{x}}{dt} = v_{\phi}(\mathbf{x}_t, t, \boldsymbol{\mu}, \mathbf{x}_{LR})
$$

The generated field is:

$$
\widehat{\mathbf{x}}_{HR} = \boldsymbol{\mu} + \widehat{\mathbf{r}}_{FM}
$$

That is why solver choices matter. Euler, Heun, number of steps, noise temperature and tiling affect the final
residual map even when the trained weights are fixed.

::: {.reference-grid}
::: {.reference-card}
### Main paper

[Flow Matching for Generative Modeling](https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.02747)
:::

::: {.reference-card}
### Useful code reference

[g4vrel/CFM](https://github.com/g4vrel/CFM)
:::

::: {.reference-card}
### OceanLens implementation

`src/oceanlens/losses/fm.py` and `src/oceanlens/models/fm_unet.py`
:::
:::

## OceanLens Choices

| Choice | Why it matters |
|---|---|
| Residual target `HR - mu` | FM only learns what CNO missed |
| Minibatch OT | Better matching between noise samples and residual targets |
| Logit-normal `t` sampling | Samples more useful intermediate times |
| Heun inference | More accurate ODE integration than Euler |
| CFG fine-tuning | Allows stronger conditioning at inference |
| Re-coarsening loss | Keeps generated HR consistent with LR after pooling |
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml:
## Role In OceanLens

Flow Matching models the unresolved residual after the CNO mean:

$$
\mathbf{x}_1 = \mathbf{x}_{HR} - \boldsymbol{\mu}
$$

The model starts from noise:

$$
\mathbf{x}_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(0, I)
$$

and learns a velocity field that moves from noise to the residual:

$$
\mathbf{x}_t = (1-t)\mathbf{x}_0 + t\mathbf{x}_1
$$

For the linear path used in the v4 family, the target velocity is:

$$
\mathbf{v}^{*} = \mathbf{x}_1 - \mathbf{x}_0
$$

The training objective is:

$$
\mathcal{L}_{FM} =
\mathbb{E}_{t,\mathbf{x}_0,\mathbf{x}_1}
\left[
\left\| v_{\phi}(\mathbf{x}_t, t, \boldsymbol{\mu}, \mathbf{x}_{LR})
- (\mathbf{x}_1 - \mathbf{x}_0) \right\|_2^2
\right]
$$

## Inference

At inference time the model samples a new residual by solving an ODE:

$$
\frac{d\mathbf{x}}{dt} = v_{\phi}(\mathbf{x}_t, t, \boldsymbol{\mu}, \mathbf{x}_{LR})
$$

The generated field is:

$$
\widehat{\mathbf{x}}_{HR} = \boldsymbol{\mu} + \widehat{\mathbf{r}}_{FM}
$$

That is why solver choices matter. Euler, Heun, number of steps, noise temperature and tiling affect the final
residual map even when the trained weights are fixed.

::: {.reference-grid}
::: {.reference-card}
### Main paper

[Flow Matching for Generative Modeling](https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.02747)
:::

::: {.reference-card}
### Useful code reference

[g4vrel/CFM](https://github.com/g4vrel/CFM)
:::

::: {.reference-card}
### OceanLens implementation

`src/oceanlens/losses/fm.py` and `src/oceanlens/models/fm_unet.py`
:::
:::

## OceanLens Choices

| Choice | Why it matters |
|---|---|
| Residual target `HR - mu` | FM only learns what CNO missed |
| Minibatch OT | Better matching between noise samples and residual targets |
| Logit-normal `t` sampling | Samples more useful intermediate times |
| Heun inference | More accurate ODE integration than Euler |
| CFG fine-tuning | Allows stronger conditioning at inference |
| Re-coarsening loss | Keeps generated HR consistent with LR after pooling |
